// base.rs - Core data processing module
// Version: 0.6.0 (branch: feature/serialization)

use std::collections::HashMap;
use std::fmt;
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};

/// Represents a data record
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct Record {
    pub id: u64,
    pub name: String,
    pub value: f64,
    pub tags: Vec<String>,
    pub metadata: HashMap<String, String>,
}

impl Record {
    pub fn new(id: u64, name: &str, value: f64) -> Self {
        Record {
            id,
            name: name.to_string(),
            value,
            tags: Vec::new(),
            metadata: HashMap::new(),
        }
    }

    pub fn add_tag(&mut self, tag: &str) {
        self.tags.push(tag.to_string());
    }

    pub fn set_meta(&mut self, key: &str, value: &str) {
        self.metadata.insert(key.to_string(), value.to_string());
    }

    pub fn get_meta(&self, key: &str) -> Option<&str> {
        self.metadata.get(key).map(|s| s.as_str())
    }

    pub fn to_json(&self) -> Result<String, serde_json::Error> {
        serde_json::to_string(self)
    }

    pub fn from_json(json: &str) -> Result<Self, serde_json::Error> {
        serde_json::from_str(json)
    }
}

impl fmt::Display for Record {
    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {
        write!(f, "Record(id={}, name={}, value={})", self.id, self.name, self.value)
    }
}

/// Processor handles batch data operations
pub struct Processor {
    records: Vec<Record>,
    batch_size: usize,
}

impl Processor {
    pub fn new(batch_size: usize) -> Self {
        Processor {
            records: Vec::new(),
            batch_size,
        }
    }

    pub fn add_record(&mut self, record: Record) {
        self.records.push(record);
    }

    pub fn process_batch(&self) -> Vec<Record> {
        self.records
            .chunks(self.batch_size)
            .flat_map(|chunk| chunk.to_vec())
            .collect()
    }

    pub fn filter_by_value(&self, threshold: f64) -> Vec<&Record> {
        self.records
            .iter()
            .filter(|r| r.value > threshold)
            .collect()
    }

    pub fn total_value(&self) -> f64 {
        self.records.iter().map(|r| r.value).sum()
    }

    pub fn average_value(&self) -> f64 {
        if self.records.is_empty() {
            return 0.0;
        }
        self.total_value() / self.records.len() as f64
    }

    pub fn group_by_tag(&self) -> HashMap<String, Vec<&Record>> {
        let mut groups: HashMap<String, Vec<&Record>> = HashMap::new();
        for record in &self.records {
            for tag in &record.tags {
                groups.entry(tag.clone()).or_default().push(record);
            }
        }
        groups
    }

    pub fn export_json(&self) -> Result<String, serde_json::Error> {
        serde_json::to_string(&self.records)
    }

    pub fn import_json(&mut self, json: &str) -> Result<usize, serde_json::Error> {
        let records: Vec<Record> = serde_json::from_str(json)?;
        let count = records.len();
        self.records.extend(records);
        Ok(count)
    }

    pub fn find_by_id(&self, id: u64) -> Option<&Record> {
        self.records.iter().find(|r| r.id == id)
    }

    pub fn find_by_meta(&self, key: &str, value: &str) -> Vec<&Record> {
        self.records
            .iter()
            .filter(|r| r.get_meta(key) == Some(value))
            .collect()
    }
}

/// Error types for processing failures
#[derive(Debug)]
pub enum ProcessError {
    InvalidData(String),
    BatchFailed(usize),
    Timeout,
    SerializationError(String),
}

impl fmt::Display for ProcessError {
    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {
        match self {
            ProcessError::InvalidData(msg) => write!(f, "Invalid data: {}", msg),
            ProcessError::BatchFailed(n) => write!(f, "Batch {} failed", n),
            ProcessError::Timeout => write!(f, "Operation timed out"),
            ProcessError::SerializationError(msg) => write!(f, "Serialization error: {}", msg),
        }
    }
}

/// Validates a record before processing
pub fn validate_record(record: &Record) -> Result<(), ProcessError> {
    if record.name.is_empty() {
        return Err(ProcessError::InvalidData("name cannot be empty".to_string()));
    }
    if record.value < 0.0 {
        return Err(ProcessError::InvalidData("value cannot be negative".to_string()));
    }
    Ok(())
}

pub fn validate_json(json: &str) -> Result<(), ProcessError> {
    serde_json::from_str::<Record>(json)
        .map(|_| ())
        .map_err(|e| ProcessError::SerializationError(e.to_string()))
}

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    #[test]
    fn test_new_record() {
        let record = Record::new(1, "test", 42.0);
        assert_eq!(record.id, 1);
        assert_eq!(record.name, "test");
        assert_eq!(record.value, 42.0);
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_record_metadata() {
        let mut record = Record::new(1, "test", 10.0);
        record.set_meta("source", "api");
        assert_eq!(record.get_meta("source"), Some("api"));
        assert_eq!(record.get_meta("missing"), None);
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_record_serialization() {
        let mut record = Record::new(1, "test", 42.0);
        record.add_tag("important");
        let json = record.to_json().unwrap();
        let restored = Record::from_json(&json).unwrap();
        assert_eq!(record, restored);
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_processor_total() {
        let mut proc = Processor::new(10);
        proc.add_record(Record::new(1, "a", 10.0));
        proc.add_record(Record::new(2, "b", 20.0));
        assert_eq!(proc.total_value(), 30.0);
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_processor_export_import() {
        let mut proc = Processor::new(10);
        proc.add_record(Record::new(1, "a", 10.0));
        proc.add_record(Record::new(2, "b", 20.0));
        let json = proc.export_json().unwrap();

        let mut proc2 = Processor::new(10);
        let count = proc2.import_json(&json).unwrap();
        assert_eq!(count, 2);
        assert_eq!(proc2.total_value(), 30.0);
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_validate_record() {
        let valid = Record::new(1, "valid", 5.0);
        assert!(validate_record(&valid).is_ok());

        let invalid = Record::new(2, "", 5.0);
        assert!(validate_record(&invalid).is_err());
    }
}
